document.title: Regional Guidelines for Codex Contact Points and National Codex Committees (Near East)
document.label: CXG 58R-2005 - Regional Guidelines for Codex Contact Points and National Codex Committees (Near East)

sections.title: CODEX CONTACT POINT DESCRIPTION
sections.text:
3. In order to facilitate efficient and effective communication and working relations between the Codex Alimentarius Commission and governments, the Commission recommends the establishment of Codex Contact Points in every Codex member country.
4. The Codex Contact Point serves as the official link between the Codex Alimentarius and the member country concerned. In essence, the Codex Contact Point serves as the official national communication agent (for and on behalf of the member country) to the Commission. This implies that all communication from the member country to the commission and vice versa, as well as within national authorities, goes through the Codex Contact Point.

3. In order to facilitate efficient and effective communication and working relations between the Codex Alimentarius Commission and governments, the Commission recommends the establishment of Codex Contact Points in every Codex member country.
4. The Codex Contact Point serves as the official link between the Codex Alimentarius and the member country concerned. In essence, the Codex Contact Point serves as the official national communication agent (for and on behalf of the member country) to the Commission. This implies that all communication from the member country to the commission and vice versa, as well as within national authorities, goes through the Codex Contact Point.

sections.title: INTRODUCTION
sections.text:
1. Codex Contact Points and National Codex Committees are amongst the essential organs of the Codex Alimentarius Commission that enhance the active involvement of various stakeholders at the national level, which translates to effective participation of Codex member countries in the activities of the Commission, resulting in a credible food code.
2. This document outlines basic guidelines for the establishment, structure, tasks and organisation of effective Codex Contact Points and National Codex Committees. Should there be existing Codex Contact Points and National Codex Committees, these guidelines could serve to further improve on their efficiency.

sections.title: LOCATION
sections.text:
6. Upon carefully reading and understanding the rule and tasks of the national Codex Contact Point, the decision on where to locate the Codex Contact Point basically rests with the member country concerned.
7. This decision may be made either by the government and/or through consultation with the relevant stakeholders at the national level.
8. However, it is common practice for the government to take up responsibility for hosting the national Codex Contact Point.

8. However, it is common practice for the government to take up responsibility for hosting the national Codex Contact Point.

sections.title: Whatever the location of the national Codex Contact Point
path_titles: Whatever the location of the national Codex Contact Point
sections.retrieval_text:
Whatever the location of the national Codex Contact Point
number=9
level=1
It is desirable that the location meets the following criteria:
- that it is as neutral as possible as regards all interested parties’ involvement in Codex work;
- that it is capable of performing the tasks of the Codex Contact Point;
- that it is accessible to all parties interested and/or those involved in the activities of Codex.

sections.title: NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEE
sections.text:
The primary objective of the National Codex Committee is to act as a consultative group to the government on matters related to Codex.
Representatives from all relevant national bodies, including consumers’ organizations, professional associations, etc. should be invited to attend meetings of the National Codex Committee to put forward their views for consideration and inclusion in the national negotiating position.

sections.title: FUNDING
sections.text: 23. The source of funding for running and/or for the activities of the Codex Contact Point and the National Codex Committee must be known to all members of the Committee. Contributions must not be attached to any conditions in order to ensure impartiality and avoid any conflict of interest, so that the two bodies can operate effectively without fear or favour.
sections.retrieval_text:
FUNDING
number=FUNDING
level=1
23. The source of funding for running and/or for the activities of the Codex Contact Point and the National Codex Committee must be known to all members of the Committee. Contributions must not be attached to any conditions in order to ensure impartiality and avoid any conflict of interest, so that the two bodies can operate effectively without fear or favour.

sections.number: NOMINATION TO THE NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEE
sections.title: NOMINATION TO THE NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEE
path_titles: NOMINATION TO THE NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEE
sections.citation: 05eedaeb-f24d-48c0-9ba2-9a01d1e923c0, Section NOMINATION TO THE NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEE, p. 3
sections.text: 21. The procedure for nomination to/inclusion in the National Codex Committee should be known, open and transparent. Different countries may use different methods to select persons to participate in the work of the Committee, depending on the available resources and structures. 22. Participation in Committee meetings should be reviewed annually, and new members appointed to replace inactive members.

level=1
21. The procedure for nomination to/inclusion in the National Codex Committee should be known, open and transparent. Different countries may use different methods to select persons to participate in the work of the Committee, depending on the available resources and structures. 22. Participation in Committee meetings should be reviewed annually, and new members appointed to replace inactive members.

page_spans.text: 21. The procedure for nomination to/inclusion in the National Codex Committee should be known, open and transparent. Different countries may use different methods to select persons to participate in the work of the Committee, depending on the available resources and structures. 22. Participation in Committee meetings should be reviewed annually, and new members appointed to replace inactive members.

sentences.original: 3. In order to facilitate efficient and effective communication and working relations between the Codex Alimentarius Commission and governments, the Commission recommends the establishment of Codex Contact Points in every Codex member country.

prefilter.reason: The sentence describes a communication process, likely relevant for normative analysis.

classification.sentence: This implies that all communication from the member country to the commission and vice versa, as well as within national authorities, goes through the Codex Contact Point.
classification.function: Prescriptive statement defining mandatory communication routing
classification.pattern: This implies that [all communication from {source} to {destination} and vice versa, as well as within {additional_entities},] goes through [designated_point].
classification.justification: The sentence prescribes a mandatory communication routing between entities, expressed indirectly by 'implies that' followed by a definitive statement. The structure does not match any known template, requiring a new pattern representing prescriptive communication pathways.
notes: The use of 'This implies that' introduces an indirect obligation rather than an explicit modal verb like 'shall' or 'must'. This may cause ambiguity in strict modality classification but is interpreted here as an obligation due to the definitive routing described.

No known templates in the catalog match this structure closely, resulting in a low match score and classification as a new pattern with a permission modality.

prefilter.reason: Contains normative language outlining desirable criteria for a location related to Codex, suitable for deeper normative analysis.

classification.pattern: Impersonal construction expressing desirability that subject meets enumerated qualitative criteria

components.subject: the location
criteria: it is as neutral as possible as regards all interested parties’ involvement in Codex work
it is capable of performing the tasks of the Codex Contact Point
it is accessible to all parties interested and/or those involved in the activities of Codex

classification.justification: The sentence uses an impersonal formulation 'It is desirable that...' expressing a non-mandatory recommendation about subject qualities, which aligns best with an obligation modality of soft normative guidance (recommendation). The enumerated qualitative criteria form the core content. No exact template matches this unique impersonal wishful structure, thus classified as new_pattern with obligation modality and recommendation function.
notes: The sentence does not assert a mandatory obligation but rather a desirable condition, indicating a softer modality.
Impersonal construction with enumeration is uncommon among strict normative templates, hence no strong match.

It combines a prohibition with a detailed purpose and a result clause, a structure not represented in known templates. Therefore, a new pattern is needed to preserve these elements accurately.
notes: The sentence combines prohibition modality with purpose and result clauses, which is a complex structure uncommon in typical templates.

sentences.original: 21. The procedure for nomination to/inclusion in the National Codex Committee should be known, open and transparent.

classification.sentence: Participation in Committee meetings should be reviewed annually, and new members appointed to replace inactive members.
classification.function: Mandate to perform specific administrative actions routinely
classification.pattern: [Subject] should be [Action 1] annually, and [Action 2] to [Purpose]
classification.justification: Sentence contains an obligation modality expressed by 'should' directing two administrative actions connected by 'and', forming a compound mandate not captured in existing templates.
notes: Sentence expresses two coordinated obligations in one sentence, which is a less common structural pattern.

pages.title: REGIONAL GUIDELINES FOR CODEX CONTACT POINTS AND NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEES (NEAR EAST)
pages.summary: Página com o título do documento e cabeçalho presentes. Identificados quatro títulos principais: INTRODUCTION, CODEX CONTACT POINT DESCRIPTION, TASKS e LOCATION. TASKS contém subtítulos listados (a) a (f) sem texto adicional. O texto está distribuído corretamente nos nós. Não há continuidade com outras páginas.
Página com vários títulos de nível 1 e subtítulos de nível 2 nos Tasks. Texto associado corretamente e uso de continuidade positiva em Tasks e Composition and Structure para seguir na próxima página.

A página contém duas seções principais de nível 1: 'NOMINATION TO THE NATIONAL CODEX COMMITTEE' e 'FUNDING'. Cada seção apresenta seu texto associado completo na página. O cabeçalho repetitivo está presente e não é incluído no texto das seções. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas para estas seções.
